tests.name: release_workflow_created
tests.details: MISSING — init.sh logged: 'No release pipeline template for platform mcp_server on host github. Skipping release pipeline.' Cause: init.sh constructs path templates/pipelines/release/github/${PLATFORM}.yml = mcp_server.yml but the actual template file is mcp-server.yml (hyphen). Affects every mcp_server-platform project.

tests.name: platform_module_present
tests.details: MISSING docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md. init.sh copies $SCRIPT_DIR/docs/platform-modules/${PLATFORM}.md (looks for mcp_server.md) but framework only ships mcp-server.md (hyphen). Same root cause as release_workflow_created — platform key 'mcp_server' uses underscore, but the framework's content files use hyphenated names.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh: mcp_server platform key never matches mcp-server template/doc filenames (underscore vs hyphen)
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash init.sh --non-interactive --platform mcp_server --language python --deployment personal --project x --project-dir <tmp> --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private --allow-existing-dir
framework_bugs.expected: Project should receive docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md (or mcp-server.md) AND .github/workflows/release.yml derived from the mcp-server.yml template.
framework_bugs.actual: Both are skipped because init.sh builds paths as ${PLATFORM}.md / ${PLATFORM}.yml using the underscore variant ('mcp_server'), but the framework ships those files with hyphens ('mcp-server.md', 'mcp-server.yml'). init.sh prints '[INFO] No platform module for mcp_server' and '[INFO] No release pipeline template for platform mcp_server on host github. Skipping release pipeline.' verify-install then warns 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing'. Affects every mcp_server-platform UAT config. Fix: normalize the key (e.g. tr '_' '-' before lookup) or rename the framework files to use underscores. init.sh:305 (platform module copy loop) and init.sh:2310 (release_template path).

documentation_gaps.gap: verify-install warns 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing → Copy from orchestrator: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md' but no such file exists in the orchestrator (only mcp-server.md). The remediation hint sends the user to a non-existent path.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 returned 0

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: .phase1_architecture.steps_completed is an array with all 5 steps after completion

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 steps (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed in order

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh returned 0; reports 'Phase gates consistent.'

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase remained 0 after completing all phase1_architecture steps and the gate check. No script in the framework auto-advances current_phase.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete every step in phase1_architecture (or any later phase) via process-checklist.sh, then run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh and re-read .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase
framework_bugs.expected: current_phase should advance from 0 to 1 (and onward) once all phase steps are complete and the gate passes. Either the gate script or a dedicated --advance-phase command should write the new value.
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remained 0 throughout the entire run, even after completing phase1_architecture, build_loop, uat_session, phase3_validation, and phase4_release. check-phase-gate.sh only verifies internal consistency; it never advances. This bug from the 2026-04-25 sweep remains open.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1' after --start-feature

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: tests_written → tests_verified_failing → implemented → security_audit (with stub findings file uat-feat-1-security-audit.md) → documentation_updated → feature_recorded all completed sequentially

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after all 5 build_loop steps; commit SHA d91aad5

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded (commit 970aba1) WITHOUT going through Build Loop. The PreToolUse hook is the only enforcement path; raw shell commits bypass it. Documented as still-open in runbook.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 and feature_recorded step, ran --start-feature uat-feat-2 then simulated PreToolUse with payload {tool_input.command: 'git commit -m "feat(uat-2): stub feature 2"'} → pre-commit-gate.sh returned no deny output (allow). Grace window correctly reset on feature_recorded (PR #17 fix holds).

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema; pre-commit-gate.sh returned a JSON deny payload referencing the pending question, options, recommendation, and offered_at fields, plus the resolve hint.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel; subsequent simulated PreToolUse commit returned no deny (allow).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json .features_completed = ["uat-feat-1"]

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a project shell (no Claude Code wrapping the command), run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(no-loop): bypass'` without --start-feature first.
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should refuse the commit when no Build Loop has been recorded for the feature, regardless of whether the commit comes from the agent or a raw shell.
framework_bugs.actual: Commit succeeded (970aba1). Enforcement only fires through the PreToolUse hook chain; raw shell commits route around it. Pre-existing limitation; no git commit-msg hook is installed by init.sh.

documentation_gaps.gap: Builder's Guide / BL-010 should document that BL-006 enforcement is PreToolUse-only and that direct shell `git commit` cannot be gated by the framework. Mention that a git commit-msg hook would be needed for raw-shell coverage.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches start-of-run SHA). git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked items (.claude/, Reports/uat-2026-04-26/) — no tracked-file modifications.

summary: Init/build/UAT/release flows all run end-to-end via --non-interactive; PR #17/#18/#19 fixes (pending-approval helper, lint-uat-scenarios helper, BL-015 sentinel reader, BL-006 grace-window reset, framework-self guard, fake-remote tolerance) all verified. One new High bug found: mcp_server platform key never matches mcp-server template/doc filenames (underscore vs hyphen) — both docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md and .github/workflows/release.yml are silently skipped on every mcp_server project. U-D (no auto-advance of current_phase) and U-C (raw shell git commit bypasses BL-006) re-confirmed as still-open prior-sweep bugs.
